inlineContent.text: This is the built-in string Variant type (and the one used by GDScript). Strings may contain any number of Unicode characters, and expose methods useful for manipulating and generating strings. Strings are reference-counted and use a copy-on-write approach (every modification to a string returns a new
), so passing them around is cheap in resources.
Some string methods have corresponding variations. Variations suffixed with
(they make no distinction between uppercase and lowercase letters). Method variations prefixed with
, etc.) are reversed, and start from the end of the string, instead of the beginning.
In a boolean context, a string will evaluate to
). Otherwise, a string will always evaluate to
should be used to check for empty strings.
